data.title: Precious Metals Surge Amid U.S. Dollar Decline, Bitcoin Gains Attention
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, precious metals are experiencing significant gains as the U.S. dollar faces debasement, with gold reaching $4,000 per ounce and silver hitting a 45-year high of over $50 per ounce. Despite this rally, there are indications that the momentum may be waning, prompting investors to consider alternative store-of-value assets such as Bitcoin (BTC) and tokenized real-world assets. Gold's impressive rally of over 50% this year, alongside a forecast by Goldman Sachs predicting a price of $4,900 per ounce by the end of 2026, suggests that the metal might be "overheated," according to Nic Puckrin, founder of the Coin Bureau education company. Puckrin noted that after such a substantial increase in gold prices, attention might shift to other alternatives that offer similar value propositions, including other metals, commodities, tokenized real assets, and Bitcoin, which are perceived as undervalued compared to gold. These assets are seen as hedges against fiat currency inflation and geopolitical uncertainty.

Bitcoin has also reached a record high of over $126,000 in October, coinciding with the historic surge in precious metals prices. This comes as investors express diminishing confidence in the U.S. dollar, which is on track for its worst performance since 1973. Market analysts at the Kobeissi Letter highlighted that the U.S. dollar is experiencing its most significant decline since 1973, having lost over 10% year-to-date and 40% of its purchasing power since 2000. The debasement of the U.S. dollar has led to a simultaneous rush into both store-of-value and risk assets, which traditionally move in opposite directions. Typically, safe-haven and store-of-value assets increase in value when risk assets like stocks decline, and vice versa.

This trend indicates that investors are adjusting asset valuations for a "new era of monetary policy," characterized by higher inflation and government financing through further currency devaluation, leading to a rise in all asset prices. Bitcoin is well-positioned to benefit in the fourth quarter due to ongoing currency debasement, as investors seek to preserve wealth by investing in safe-haven assets, according to Matt Hougan, chief investment officer at investment firm Bitwise.

data.title: Cryptocurrency hearings expected to continue during US government shutdown: Source
data.description: According to people familiar with the matter, the leadership of the U.S. Senate Banking Committee hopes to complete the revision of the Responsible Financial Innovation Act by the end of this month. The House of Representatives passed the Financial Transparency Act in July, after which the Senate Banking Committee and the Agriculture Committee each developed bills, which will later be merged. Republicans on the Banking Committee released a revised version last month, and Democrats provided a list of principles they hope the legislation will include. The Agriculture Committee's draft has not yet been released, and it may take longer to complete. Although the U.S. Senate once again rejected two plans to provide funding for the federal government to end the nine-day shutdown, and the cryptocurrency policy agenda has been shelved due to an uncertain budget, Washington insiders feel that the momentum has not completely disappeared. The House of Representatives has suspended work due to the shutdown, and the Senate is still meeting on budget and cryptocurrency-related issues. (Blockworks)

data.title: U.S. Government Shutdown Impacts Cryptocurrency Policy Progress
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the U.S. government shutdown has entered its ninth day, creating uncertainty around the cryptocurrency policy agenda due to budget constraints. Despite the House of Representatives being in recess, the Senate continues to hold sessions on budget and cryptocurrency matters.

Insiders report that the Senate Banking Committee remains committed to advancing cryptocurrency market structure policies, even if it requires holding hearings during the shutdown. They aim to conduct a markup of the Responsible Financial Innovation Act by the end of the month, regardless of budget approval. Before the shutdown, the draft was nearly complete, but reduced federal agency staff is expected to delay progress. Most agency staff collaborating with congressional committees on policies like cryptocurrency market structure are on temporary leave, decreasing the likelihood of a swift release of the draft.

With limited federal agency personnel, new spot cryptocurrency ETFs are unlikely to be approved before the shutdown ends. The SEC's shutdown operations plan indicates that the agency will not review or approve registration statements or issue effectiveness notices. However, given the SEC's new general listing standards and the significant pre-shutdown efforts by issuers and agency staff, two issuers express confidence that new products will be quickly approved once federal operations resume.

data.title: CZ: Memecoin market sentiment may fluctuate rapidly, but the ecosystem will not change in a day
data.description: CZ responded to the market volatility on the X platform, stating that the market decline appeared to be due to false information (FUD) about Alpha not listing certain tokens in the future. Although this was later disproven, it had already had an impact on the market. This shows that when enough people are misled by false information, FUD can still have a destructive impact on the market. He also emphasized that he was not involved in the relevant incident. CZ added that market sentiment can fluctuate rapidly (especially in meme coins), but the ecosystem will not change in a day.
